normalized_title: City of Fremantle Local Planning Policy 2.2 - Split Density Codes and Energy Efficiency and Sustainability Schedule
version_date: Adopted 17 October 2007 (C0710-04); amended 16 April 2014 (PSC1404-74) and 19 January 2022 (PC2201-10)
scope_summary: Implements clause 4.3.4 of Local Planning Scheme No. 4 for sites with a split residential density code (e.g. R35/R40) connected to reticulated sewerage. The higher density code may only be applied via one of four pathways: retention of a building of cultural heritage significance, provision of 'low income housing', compliance with the City's Energy Efficiency and Sustainability Schedule (Part B), or removal of a non-conforming use; otherwise the lower code prevails. Applies to new dwelling development on split-coded land, not to renovations/additions to existing dwellings.

key_numeric_standards.unit: NatHERS star above current BCA class 1A requirement
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: new dwellings seeking the higher density code via the Energy Efficiency and Sustainability Schedule

key_numeric_standards.topic: photovoltaic solar panel system minimum capacity
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: new dwellings under the Energy Efficiency and Sustainability Schedule
tank plumbed to toilet or laundry (alternatives: approved greywater reuse system, or registration and retention of a significant tree)
where Part B cl 1.2 and/or 1.3 cannot reasonably be met

definitions: building of cultural heritage significance (Part A cl 1.1)
low income housing (as defined in Schedule 1 of the Scheme)
non-conforming use (as defined in the Scheme)

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
Building Codes of Australia (class 1A energy efficiency requirement)
City's Significant Tree and Vegetation Areas Register (LPP 2.23)
AS4970-2009 Protection of Trees on Development Sites
